channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rubio and Witkoff met Ukrainian and European officials for landmark talks on Ukraine's future
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France hosted high-level talks Thursday to discuss Ukraine and its security, the first time since President Donald Trump's inauguration that top American, Ukrainian and European officials are known to have met together to discuss an end to the war. U.S.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Draft budget plan proposes deep cuts across federal health programs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A draft budget proposal circulating among federal officials would dramatically deepen cuts at the nation's top health agency, eliminating some public health programs entirely and serving as a roadmap for more mass firings. The document suggests a cut in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge will wait to rule on an effort to block Trump's executive order on elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday said she would wait to decide whether to grant a preliminary injunction to national Democrats and voting rights groups that are challenging President Donald Trump’s recent executive order on elections. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's in 'no rush' to end tariffs as he meets with Italy's Meloni
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday he is in “no rush” to reach any trade deals because he views tariffs as making the United States wealthy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and COLLEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barack Obama, Bernie Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: In one YouGov survey on Democratic figures, 58 percent of respondents said they felt “very favorable” or “somewhat favorable” toward Obama.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Moderate Republicans draw red line on Medicaid cuts in Trump agenda bill
item.description: Vulnerable and moderate House Republicans are warning House GOP leaders that they will not support the “one big, beautiful bill” encompassing President Trump’s legislative agenda if it includes cuts to Medicaid benefits.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: New bill could allow for sale of wine at New York grocery stores
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (WIVB) — A new bill recently introduced in the New York State Senate could soon allow for the sale of wine in grocery stores across New York State.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, PIX 11

item.title: Clashing Over Campus Control: Lawmakers vs. DeSantis on transparency
item.description: For years, lawmakers have speculated that the leaders who run the state's Universities and Colleges have been gifted these positions based on their political loyalties to the Governor.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida House votes to stop pickleball courts, hotels from being built in state parks
item.description: A bill aiming to protect Florida's state parks from overdevelopment was unanimously passed Wednesday by the state House.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protest of Lombardo's 'freeloaders' comment planned Saturday in downtown Las Vegas
item.description: A rally planned Saturday afternoon in downtown Las Vegas is stepping up the pushback against Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's remarks that freeloaders are behind opposition to policy changes.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's wife sought restraining order in 2021, DHS says
item.description: The wife of Kilmar Abrego Garcia filed for an order of protection against her husband in 2021, saying he was violent toward her.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: PIX on Politics Daily: NYC mayoral election poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Welcome to PIX on Politics Daily with Dan Mannarino, where we break down the day's political news, headlines, and issues that matter most to you through in-depth conversation. Join us daily on PIX11+ streaming at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: DOJ suing Maine amid Title IX, trans athlete dispute
item.description: Maine's governor says a state law barring discrimination based on gender identity supersedes Trump's executive order.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Oler, NewsNation

item.title: States push Medicaid work rules, but few programs help enrollees find jobs
item.description: Some lawmakers are pushing to require that Medicaid recipients work in order to get or keep coverage, and some states already try to help them find jobs. But the effects of those efforts are unclear.
item.dc:creator: Sam Whitehead, Phil Galewitz, and Katheryn Houghton, MPR News

item.title: Trump plans order to cut funding for NPR and PBS
item.description: President Trump and GOP members of Congress have accused the public broadcasters of biased and "woke" programming. The president plans a rescission, which would give Congress 45 days to approve the directive or allow funding to be restored.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik and Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: In Pelican Rapids, teens learn the skill of civil discourse
item.description: In a deeply polarized country, a new program in Minnesota is teaching middle and high schoolers how to find common ground in their communities. Program leaders say it’s an effort to inspire young people to become engaged politically and in their towns.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert, MPR News

item.title: Former President Joe Biden to speak in Chicago on Tuesday in effort to rally support for Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden is scheduled to speak at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9
